content: The orthographic variant spelling `reflexion` (the older, less common spelling of reflection taught in high school English vocabulary) can be framed as a natural 'perspective perturbation' for LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where prompting an LLM to generate responses under the archaic spelling primes inconsistent solving perspectives that improve self-correction. This builds on existing work that leverages inconsistent perspectives like Self-Contrast to boost reflection quality.
